records.ety: [L. delegatus, p. p. of delegare to send, delegate; de- + legare to send with a commission, to depute. See Legate.]

defs: Any one sent and empowered to act for another; one deputed to represent; a chosen deputy; a representative; a commissioner; a vicar.
One elected by the people of a territory to represent them in Congress, where he has the right of debating, but not of voting.
One sent by any constituency to act as its representative in a convention; as, a delegate to a convention for nominating officers, or for forming or altering a constitution.
Sent to act for or represent another; deputed; as, a delegate judge.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Delegated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Delegating (?).]

defs: To send as one's representative; to empower as an ambassador; to send with power to transact business; to commission; to depute; to authorize.
To intrust to the care or management of another; to transfer; to assign; to commit.
quotes: The delegated administration of the law.
The power exercised by the legislature is the people's power, delegated by the people to the legislative.
